newey_west_cov <- function(model, lag = 30) {
  # Design matrix (including intercept)
  X <- model.matrix(model)
  # Residuals from the model
  u <- residuals(model)
  T <- length(u)
  
  # S0 = Σ u_t^2 * x_t %*% t(x_t)
  S <- crossprod(u * X)
  
  # Add lagged autocovariance terms with Bartlett weights
  for (l in seq_len(lag)) {
    w <- 1 - l / (lag + 1)               # Bartlett weight
    # Cross‑product for lag l
    S_l <- crossprod(
      u[(l + 1):T] * X[(l + 1):T, , drop = FALSE],
      u[1:(T - l)] * X[1:(T - l), , drop = FALSE]
    )
    S <- S + w * (S_l + t(S_l))
  }
  
  # HAC variance‑covariance matrix
  XtX_inv <- solve(crossprod(X))
  vcov_hac <- XtX_inv %*% S %*% XtX_inv
  vcov_hac
}

# Ensure the model object is named `fit` (rename if necessary)
fit <- lm_ret   # lm_ret was created in step 7

# Newey‑West HAC covariance matrix (30‑day lag)
vcov_nw <- newey_west_cov(fit, lag = 30)

# Robust standard errors
se_nw <- sqrt(diag(vcov_nw))

# Coefficient estimates
coef_nw <- coef(fit)

# Assemble results
nw_results <- data.frame(
  estimate   = coef_nw,
  std_error  = se_nw,
  row.names  = names(coef_nw)
)

# Clean up temporary objects that are no longer needed
rm(vcov_nw, se_nw, coef_nw)

# Re‑fit the regression (Bitcoin 30‑day return ~ SPX 30‑day return)
fit <- lm(btc_ret30 ~ spx_ret30, data = spx_daily_5y)

newey_west_cov <- function(model, lag = 30) {
  # Design matrix (including intercept)
  X <- model.matrix(model)
  # Model residuals
  u <- residuals(model)
  T <- length(u)

  # Initialize with the contemporaneous (lag‑0) term
  S <- crossprod(u * X)

  # Add lagged autocovariance terms with Bartlett weights
  for (l in seq_len(lag)) {
    w <- 1 - l / (lag + 1)               # Bartlett weight
    S_l <- crossprod(
      u[(l + 1):T] * X[(l + 1):T, , drop = FALSE],
      u[1:(T - l)] * X[1:(T - l), , drop = FALSE]
    )
    S <- S + w * (S_l + t(S_l))
  }

  # HAC variance‑covariance matrix
  XtX_inv <- solve(crossprod(X))
  vcov_hac <- XtX_inv %*% S %*% XtX_inv
  vcov_hac
}

# Fit the linear model: Bitcoin 30‑day return ~ SPX 30‑day return
fit <- lm(btc_ret30 ~ spx_ret30, data = spx_daily_5y)

# Compute the Newey‑West HAC covariance matrix (lag = 30)
vcov_nw <- newey_west_cov(fit, lag = 30)

# Robust standard errors (square‑root of the diagonal of the HAC covariance matrix)
se_nw <- sqrt(diag(vcov_nw))

# Coefficient estimates from the fitted model
coef_nw <- coef(fit)

# t‑statistics: estimate divided by its robust SE
t_vals <- coef_nw / se_nw

# Residual degrees of freedom from the fitted model
df_resid <- df.residual(fit)

# Two‑sided p‑values for each coefficient
p_vals <- 2 * pt(-abs(t_vals), df = df_resid)

# Create a data frame with all statistics; preserve the coefficient names as row names
nw_results <- data.frame(
  estimate   = coef_nw,
  std_error = se_nw,
  t_value   = t_vals,
  p_value   = p_vals,
  row.names = names(coef_nw)
)

# Clean up intermediate objects that are no longer needed
rm(coef_nw, se_nw, t_vals, p_vals, df_resid, vcov_nw, fit)

**Add a text annotation with the Newey‑West coefficient estimates to the plot**

```r
# Extract Newey‑West results (already stored in `nw_results`)
int_est  <- nw_results["(Intercept)", "estimate"]
int_se   <- nw_results["(Intercept)", "std_error"]
int_p    <- nw_results["(Intercept)", "p_value"]

spx_est  <- nw_results["spx_ret30", "estimate"]
spx_se   <- nw_results["spx_ret30", "std_error"]
spx_p    <- nw_results["spx_ret30", "p_value"]

# Build a single label string (two lines)
coeff_label <- paste0(
  "Intercept = ", round(int_est, 4),
  " (SE = ", round(int_se, 4), ", p = ", signif(int_p, 3), ")\n",
  "SPX 30‑day = ", round(spx_est, 4),
  " (SE = ", round(spx_se, 4), ", p = ", signif(spx_p, 3), ")"
)

# Add the annotation to the existing ggplot object
plot_nw <- plot_nw +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = Inf, y = Inf,               # place in the top‑right corner
    label = coeff_label,
    hjust = 1, vjust = 1,           # right‑align / top‑align
    size = 4,
    color = "black"
  )
```
